items.title: Meta inks deal for solar power at night, beamed from space
items.summary: Overview Energy's first contract with Meta is a small step toward a future of space-based solar power.

items.title: Truecaller faces mounting pressures as its growth matures
items.summary: As growth slows, Truecaller is leaning on subscriptions, business services, and new features to sustain momentum beyond India.

items.title: The Stanford freshmen who want to rule the world . . . will probably read this book and try even harder
items.summary: Can a book like this actually change anything? Or does the spotlight, as it always seems to, send more students racing to the place?

items.title: Amazon’s new podcast strategy: Monetize everything
items.summary: Amazon's podcasting business seems to have transformed over the past six months.

items.title: What Tim Cook built
items.summary: On the latest episode of Equity, we discuss how Apple has changed since Cook became CEO in 2011, and what challenges incoming CEO John Ternus will be facing.

items.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Elon’s admission
items.summary: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility — your central hub for news and insights on the future of transportation.

items.title: To buy this Bay Area home, you’ll need Anthropic equity
items.summary: Someone’s offering an unusual deal for a 13-acre property in Mill Valley, just north of South Francisco.

items.title: SpeakOn’s dictation device is a good idea marred by platform limitations
items.summary: This $129 device uses MagSafe to stick on the back of an iPhone to power transcription across apps

items.title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
items.summary: In a recent experiment, Anthropic created a classified marketplace where AI agents represented both buyers and sellers, striking real deals for real goods and real money.

items.title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
items.summary: L.D. 307 would have imposed the country’s first statewide moratorium on new data centers — lasting, in this case, until November 1, 2027.

items.title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
items.summary: In a letter to the residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said he is “deeply sorry” that his company failed to alert law enforcement about the suspect in a recent mass shooting.

items.title: The climate tech IPO window could finally be cracking open
items.summary: Nuclear startup X-energy went public, geothermal startup Fervo is about to. Could this be the moment that climate tech investors have been waiting for?

items.title: Why Cohere is merging with Aleph Alpha
items.summary: Canadian AI startup Cohere is taking over Germany-based Aleph Alpha with support from Lidl’s owner, Schwarz Group. With the blessing of their governments, the companies intend to offer a sovereign alternative to enterprises in an AI landscape dominated by American players.

items.title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
items.summary: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding these technologies globally.

items.title: Apps to distract you from the endless cycle of doomscrolling
items.summary: It's hard to break the cycle of doomscrolling, but there are plenty of apps that can help you spend more time on content that’s engaging and productive.

items.title: Apple under Ternus: what comes next for the tech giant’s hardware strategy
items.summary: John Ternus, Apple's incoming CEO, is a hardware guy, signaling Apple may be putting devices back at the center of its strategy.

items.title: India’s Snabbit seeks fresh funding at a $400M valuation, sources say
items.summary: Snabbit has scaled rapidly, crossing one million jobs in March, amid growing investor interest.

items.title: Lachy Groom to back India startup Pronto at a $200M valuation, sources say
items.summary: This round, should it occur, would double the house-help startup's valuation in a matter of weeks.

items.title: Steve Ballmer blasts founder he backed who pleaded guilty to fraud: ‘I was duped and feel silly’
items.summary: Steve Ballmer wrote a fiery letter in the sentencing of disgraced founder Joseph Sanberg documenting all the harm that's befalling him as an investor.

items.title: Palantir is reportedly helping the IRS investigate financial crimes
items.summary: The IRS has used Palantir's software since at least 2018, The Intercept reports.
